response: {"calibration_score":0.31,"overconfident_count":3,"underconfident_count":1,"well_calibrated_count":9,"biases_detected":["anchoring — repeated assumption that services (ports 3006/5006) and env files were present at expected paths before verifying; unit-20 made 5+ failed tool calls before confirming nothing was running","sunk_cost — unit-8 continued investing in the heredoc-based jq script approach (Write failed twice, Bash failed once) rather than pivoting to a simpler Write-then-Bash sequence earlier; 35+ tool calls for a task that resolved with a clean Write","confirmation_bias — unit-29 drilled into playwright trace binary after log grep confirmed a failure, but read .trace line-by-line rather than extracting the specific action frame that would confirm the root cause; gathered more evidence than needed to reach a conclusion already supported","scope_creep — unit-57 performed 5 WebSearch calls for a documentation task where existing repo files and prior session notes contained sufficient pattern data; external search added latency without materially changing the output"],"recommendations":["pre-flight environment check before any multi-step workflow: one lsof/pm2/ls probe batch at the start of a unit rather than discovering missing state mid-execution across multiple failed calls","for shell scripts written to /tmp, prefer Write-then-Bash over heredoc-in-Bash; the heredoc path is fragile under the tool sandbox and caused the unit-8 retry spiral","cap trace/log deep-reads at the first frame that confirms the hypothesis; in unit-29 the root cause (onSuccess routing to error branch) was visible in the first 30 lines of the grep result but reading continued for 4 more tool calls","when context-restoring (unit-47 pattern), read WAL checkpoint first and only read additional files if the checkpoint is stale or ambiguous — unit-47 read 9 files sequentially where the checkpoint + git log would have been sufficient"]}
